name: Movie File Out TOP
description: The Movie File Out TOP saves a TOP stream out to a movie file (.mov/.mp4) using a variety of codecs, including the H.264/H.265, Hap Q, NotchLC, Apple ProRes and Animation video codecs. It can also save single frame images, image sequences, or stop-frame movies.
summary: The Movie File Out TOP saves a TOP stream out to a movie file (.mov/.mp4) using a variety of codecs, including the H.264/H.265, Hap Q, NotchLC, Apple ProRes and Animation video codecs. It can also sav

parameters.name: Type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: Output either a movie, image, image sequence, or stop-frame movie.

parameters.name: Movie
parameters.label: Movie
parameters.description: movie - Records a movie file. image - Records a single image. imagesequence - Records a sequence of images. stopframemovie -

parameters.name: Video Codec
parameters.label: Video Codec
parameters.description: Select the video compression codec used to encode the movie.

parameters.name: Animation
parameters.label: Animation
parameters.description: Animation parameter.

parameters.name: Video Codec Type
parameters.label: Video Codec Type
parameters.description: videocodectype - Some video codecs such as Apple ProRes, Hap and Hap Q have a various different types such as ProRes 442 HQ, ProRe 4444 HQ etc.

parameters.name: Image File Type
parameters.label: Image File Type
parameters.description:
imagefiletype -  - Choose what file type to use when  is set to Image.

 tiff - A lossless, compressed, image format that includes alpha. jpeg - A lossy image format, very well compressed. No support for alpha. bmp - A lossless, uncompressed, image format that includes alpha. exr - A lossless, compressed, image format that can save files in 16-bit float and 32-bit float formats. Can also include alpha. More information here. png - A lossless, compressed, image format that can include alpha. Supports bit 8-bit and 16-bit fixed data. dds - A lossless, uncompressed, image format that can include alpha. Can include mipmap information. Natively support most pixel formats supported by TOPs.

parameters.name: TIFF
parameters.label: TIFF
parameters.description: tiff - A lossless, compressed, image format that includes alpha. jpeg - A lossy image format, very well compressed. No support for alpha. bmp - A lossless, uncompressed, image format that includes alpha. exr - A lossless, compressed, image format that can save files in 16-bit float and 32-bit float formats. Can also include alpha. More information here. png - A lossless, compressed, image format that can include alpha. Supports bit 8-bit and 16-bit fixed data. dds - A lossless, uncompressed, image format that can include alpha. Can include mipmap information. Natively support most pixel formats supported by TOPs.

parameters.name: Unique Suffix
parameters.label: Unique Suffix
parameters.description: uniquesuff - When enabled, me.fileSuffix will be a unique suffix when used in the file parameter.

parameters.name: N
parameters.label: N
parameters.description: n - N is the index used in me.fileSuffix. When unique suffix is enabled, N specifies the starting index to increment from when calculating a unique suffix/name.

parameters.name: File
parameters.label: File
parameters.description: file - Sets the path and filename of the movie file that is saved out. The filename must include the file extension such as .mov/.mp4 etc. For movies, generally the .mov file extension will work with the most codecs.

parameters.name: Movie Pixel Format
parameters.label: Movie Pixel Format
parameters.description:
moviepixelformat -  - Options for the pixel format based on the  selected. Use this parameter to change the color quality of the output (how many bits are used, YUV sampling etc.), as well as selecting formats that include alpha for codecs that support alpha.

 yuv420 - (Photo/Motion JPEG, MPEG 4 (Part 2), ) yuv420 - (H.264 (NVIDIA ), H.265/HEVC (NVIDIA )) yuv420p10bit - (H.265/HEVC (NVIDIA )) yuv422 - (Photo/Motion JPEG, ) yuv444 - (H.264 (NVIDIA ), H.265/HEVC (NVIDIA )) yuv444p10bit - (H.265/HEVC (NVIDIA )) rgb - (Animation, Hap, Hap Q) rgba - (Animation, GoPro-Cineform, Hap, Hap Q) rgba - (Hap Q) - Slow, Read Help Before Using! 3 - (GIF)

parameters.name: YUV 4:2:0
parameters.label: YUV 4:2:0
parameters.description: yuv420 - (Photo/Motion JPEG, MPEG 4 (Part 2), ) yuv420 - (H.264 (NVIDIA ), H.265/HEVC (NVIDIA )) yuv420p10bit - (H.265/HEVC (NVIDIA )) yuv422 - (Photo/Motion JPEG, ) yuv444 - (H.264 (NVIDIA ), H.265/HEVC (NVIDIA )) yuv444p10bit - (H.265/HEVC (NVIDIA )) rgb - (Animation, Hap, Hap Q) rgba - (Animation, GoPro-Cineform, Hap, Hap Q) rgba - (Hap Q) - Slow, Read Help Before Using! 3 - (GIF)

parameters.name: Audio CHOP
parameters.label: Audio CHOP
parameters.description: audiochop - Specify a  to use as the audio track for the movie. Drag & Drop a  here or manually enter the 's path. The  needs to be time-sliced.

parameters.name: Audio Codec
parameters.label: Audio Codec
parameters.description:
audiocodec -  - Select the audio compression codec used to encode the audio.

 alac - A lossless audio codec that still offers some compression for the data. mp3 - Highly compressed lossy codec. Can only do 2 channels of audio. MP3 compression will not have gapless playback. pcm16 - Uncompressed audio ( Code Modulation) pcm24 - Uncompressed audio ( Code Modulation) pcm32 - Uncompressed audio ( Code Modulation) vorbis - Vorbis is a lossy audio compression codec. Vorbis compression will have gapless playback.

parameters.name: ALAC (Apple Lossless)
parameters.label: ALAC (Apple Lossless)
parameters.description: alac - A lossless audio codec that still offers some compression for the data. mp3 - Highly compressed lossy codec. Can only do 2 channels of audio. MP3 compression will not have gapless playback. pcm16 - Uncompressed audio ( Code Modulation) pcm24 - Uncompressed audio ( Code Modulation) pcm32 - Uncompressed audio ( Code Modulation) vorbis - Vorbis is a lossy audio compression codec. Vorbis compression will have gapless playback.

parameters.name: Audio Bit Rate
parameters.label: Audio Bit Rate
parameters.description:
audiobitrate -  - The bitrate to write the audio out at.
Note: A particlular behavior on Windows OS AAC encoder; for 1 and 2 channelsthe selction of bit rate is for both channels ie. it’s 192kb/s for either of them, so either 1x192kb/s or 2x96kb/s. However, when using more than 2 channels the bitrate is per channel ie. for 6 channel then it’s 6x192kb/s.


 b96 - b128 - b192 - b256 - b320 -

parameters.name: Quality
parameters.label: Quality
parameters.description: quality - Select the quality of the movie compression. NOTE: Some codecs can not output lossless compression.

parameters.name: Movie FPS
parameters.label: Movie FPS
parameters.description: fps - The frame rate of the movie file created.

parameters.name: Limit Length
parameters.label: Limit Length
parameters.description: limitlength - This can be used to automatically stop recording the file once it reaches a specified length.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: length - The length to stop recording the file at.

parameters.name: Length Unit
parameters.label: Length Unit
parameters.description:
lengthunit -  - The unit the length is specified in.

 indices - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: I
parameters.label: I
parameters.description: indices - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: Record
parameters.label: Record
parameters.description: record - When this parameter is set to 1, the movie will be recording.

parameters.name: Pause
parameters.label: Pause
parameters.description: pause - Pauses the recording.

parameters.name: Add Frame
parameters.label: Add Frame
parameters.description: addframe - Adds a single frame to the output for each click of the button.  Pause must be On to enable the Add Frame parameter.

parameters.name: Max Threads
parameters.label: Max Threads
parameters.description: maxthread - When outputting sequences of images, this controls the maximum number of threads can be used to output images (one thread per image). If this is set to 1 then the main thread will be used to write the image.

parameters.name: Header Source DAT
parameters.label: Header Source DAT
parameters.description: headerdat - The path to a  that stores header metadata that should be written to the output image or movie file. Header data is written as key-value pairs with the first column storing the keys and the second column storing the associated values. Note: Currently only supported for EXR files. Warning: Files may fail to save if the header data conflicts with system headers.

parameters.name: Save as Point Cloud
parameters.label: Save as Point Cloud
parameters.description: pointcloud - When enabled, an additional header will be added to the file that indicates the contents are point data rather than images. This header is used to automatically load the file into a Point File In TOP rather than a Movie File In TOP when dragging and dropping.

parameters.name: Additional Input TOP
parameters.label: Additional Input TOP
parameters.description:
input - Sequence of TOPs containing image data to add to the file



  input0r - Name assigned to the specified 's Red channel.



  input0g - Name assigned to the specified 's Green channel.



  input0b - Name assigned to the specified 's Blue channel.



  input0a - Name assigned to the specified 's Alpha channel.



  input1top - The path to the  used to specify addition channels to the EXR file. The first 4 channels specified above come from the input connected to the Movie File Out , this TOPs RGBA data can be assigned unique names in the EXR file using the Red, Green, Blue, Alpha parameters below.

parameters.name: Red
parameters.label: Red
parameters.description: input0r - Name assigned to the specified 's Red channel.

parameters.name: Green
parameters.label: Green
parameters.description: input0g - Name assigned to the specified 's Green channel.

parameters.name: Blue
parameters.label: Blue
parameters.description: input0b - Name assigned to the specified 's Blue channel.

parameters.name: Alpha
parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description: input0a - Name assigned to the specified 's Alpha channel.

parameters.name: TOP
parameters.label: TOP
parameters.description: input1top - The path to the  used to specify addition channels to the EXR file. The first 4 channels specified above come from the input connected to the Movie File Out , this TOPs RGBA data can be assigned unique names in the EXR file using the Red, Green, Blue, Alpha parameters below.

parameters.name: Stall for File Open
parameters.label: Stall for File Open
parameters.description: stallforopen - When this is on playback will stall until the file is opened and ready to receive frames, to make sure the frame that was inputted when Record was turned on gets recorded. When this is off recording may start on a later frame, after the file has been opened. Turning this off can avoid a stall in playback, if missing recording some frames at the start is acceptable.

parameters.name: Profile
parameters.label: Profile
parameters.description:
profile -  - Select the H.264 profile to use.

 autoselect - baseline - main - high -

parameters.name: Auto-Select
parameters.label: Auto-Select
parameters.description: autoselect - baseline - main - high -

parameters.name: Preset
parameters.label: Preset
parameters.description:
preset -  - The H264 preset to use.

 none - Select from the available presets. lossless -

parameters.name: None
parameters.label: None
parameters.description: none - Select from the available presets. lossless -

parameters.name: Bit Rate Mode
parameters.label: Bit Rate Mode
parameters.description:
bitratemode -  - Select between Constant or Variable bit rate, and regular or high quality bit rate modes.

 constant - variable - constanthq - variablehq -

parameters.name: Constant (CBR)
parameters.label: Constant (CBR)
parameters.description: constant - variable - constanthq - variablehq -

parameters.name: Average Bitrate (Kb/s)
parameters.label: Average Bitrate (Kb/s)
parameters.description: avgbitrate - Set the average bitrate target for the encoding.

parameters.name: Peak Bitrate (Kb/s)
parameters.label: Peak Bitrate (Kb/s)
parameters.description: peakbitrate - Set the peak bitrate allowed for the encoding.

parameters.name: Keyframe Interval
parameters.label: Keyframe Interval
parameters.description: keyframeinterval - Set the number of frames between key-frames (I-frames) while encoding.

parameters.name: Max B-Frames
parameters.label: Max B-Frames
parameters.description: maxbframes - Controls the maximum number of B-frames (bi-directional frames) that will be created between pairs of key-frames.

parameters.name: Motion Prediction
parameters.label: Motion Prediction
parameters.description:
motionpredict -  - Controls the quality of the Motion Prediction used when encoding H264/H265.

 default - quarter - half - full -

parameters.name: Default
parameters.label: Default
parameters.description: default - quarter - half - full -

parameters.name: Frame Slicing
parameters.label: Frame Slicing
parameters.description: frameslicing - Controls if H264/H265 frames are sliced into multiple pieces, allowing them to be decoded using multiple CPUs more easily.

parameters.name: Num Slices
parameters.label: Num Slices
parameters.description: numslices - The number of slices each frame is split into.

parameters.name: Entropy Mode
parameters.label: Entropy Mode
parameters.description:
entropymode -  - Controls which entropy mode is used for H265 encoding.

 autoselect - cabac - cavlc -

parameters.name: Secondary Compression
parameters.label: Secondary Compression
parameters.description: secondarycompression - Hap uses a secondary CPU compression stage usually. Encoding video without this compression will result in faster playback, but potentially larger file sizes (which would require faster drives to play back).

parameters.name: Encode Test Mode
parameters.label: Encode Test Mode
parameters.description: encodetestmode - This mode disables file writting, and only does the encoding. This is useful to test the /CPU performance of encoding while taking the SSD speed out of the equation.

parameters.name: Include Mip Maps
parameters.label: Include Mip Maps
parameters.description: mipmaps - When saving out .dds file, mipmaps can be included if this is enabled. This is primarily used for the PreFilter Map TOP, which will encode special information into the mipmap levels of the texture which needs to be maintained.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. Making this larger than 1 is essentially the same as taking the output from each pass, and passing it into the first input of the node and repeating the process. Other inputs and parameters remain the same for each pass.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
